name: Reynolds Number (Re)
desc: Ratio of inertial forces to viscous forces. Predicts if flow is laminar or turbulent.
validations: 📌 For non-circular pipes, replace D with Hydraulic Diameter (Dh = 4 * Area / Perimeter).
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: 'D' is Diameter, NOT Radius! 'v' must be the average cross-sectional velocity.

name: Darcy Friction Factor (f_D)
desc: Used directly in the Darcy-Weisbach equation for head loss.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: f_D = 4 * f_Fanning. Always check which 'f' the question is asking for! Many lose 2 marks here.

name: Prandtl Number (Pr)
desc: Ratio of momentum diffusivity to thermal diffusivity.
validations: 📌 It is a property of the fluid, independent of flow conditions.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: For liquid metals, Pr << 1. For highly viscous oils, Pr >> 1.

name: Terminal Settling Velocity (Stokes' Law)
desc: Maximum constant velocity reached by a falling particle in a fluid.
validations: 📌 Strictly valid ONLY for creeping flow (Particle Reynolds Number, Re_p < 0.1).
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Don't forget to square the particle diameter (Dp)! Also, ensure viscosity is in Pa·s.

name: Log Mean Temperature Difference (LMTD)
desc: Used to determine the temperature driving force for heat transfer in flow systems.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: If ΔT1 = ΔT2 (e.g., in phase change), the formula becomes undefined (0/0). Use AMTD directly!

name: Design Equation for CSTR
desc: Relates reactor volume to conversion for a Continuous Stirred Tank Reactor.
validations: 📌 The reaction rate (-r_A) MUST be evaluated at EXIT conditions.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Do not integrate! The composition inside a CSTR is uniform and identical to the exit stream.

name: Gibbs Phase Rule
desc: Calculates the degrees of freedom (F) for non-reacting systems.
validations: 📌 C = Number of components, P = Number of phases.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: If pressure OR temperature is already fixed in the question, use F = C - P + 1.

name: Biot Number (Bi)
desc: Ratio of internal conductive resistance to external convective resistance.
validations: 📌 L_c is Characteristic Length (Volume / Surface Area).
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Do not confuse with Nusselt! In Bi, 'k' is for the SOLID. In Nu, 'k' is for the FLUID. If Bi < 0.1, use Lumped Capacitance.

name: Sherwood Number (Sh)
desc: Ratio of convective mass transfer to diffusive mass transfer.
validations: 📌 It is the mass transfer analogue of the Nusselt Number.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Watch units! k_c is the mass transfer coefficient (m/s), D_AB is the molecular diffusivity (m²/s).

name: Damköhler Number (Da)
desc: Relates the chemical reaction timescale to the transport phenomena timescale.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Da >> 1 means the system is MASS TRANSFER limited. Da << 1 means it is REACTION RATE limited.

name: Schmidt Number (Sc)
desc: Ratio of momentum diffusivity (kinematic viscosity) to mass diffusivity.
validations: 📌 Mass transfer analogue of the Prandtl Number.
trap: ⚠️ TRAP: Used to characterize flows where there are simultaneous momentum and mass diffusion processes.
